text:
## 2. Membership is per-node; **awareness inherits downward**.

The fork I flagged — here's my call:

- You are a *member* only of channels you were explicitly added to.
- You have *awareness* (fabric snapshot visibility, deltas) of all descendants of channels you're a member of.
- @-mentioning someone from a subchannel they're not in works and reads as what it is: a cross-channel mention, using your existing `@slug@backend` addressing. Replying/joining is the explicit `invite`/`switch` you already locked in decision (C) — no membership side-effects from mentions.

Why this way: inheritance-of-membership makes every subchannel roster huge and meaningless (14 members in `#tenex-edge` → 14 ghosts in every task room). Per-node membership with inherited *visibility* keeps rosters truthful while killing the re-invite tax — you can see the subchannel exists and who's there before deciding to enter. This also composes cleanly with the who/awareness format you've already shipped (subtree presence deltas already do most of this). [...] **Sequencing:** file it as one GitHub issue with call #2 (membership vs. awareness) stated as the locked decision, since it's the only genuinely irreversible semantic. #1, #3, #4 are mechanical once #2 is fixed. Want me to draft that issue? [...] Labeled `refactor:architecture`, `needs-human-policy` (the membership-vs-awareness call in decision #2 is flagged as the one semantic that needs your ratification before the mechanical work starts), and `risk:high`.
